content.problemStatement:
Consider a permutation P=(p1,p2,...,pn)P = (p_1, p_2, ..., p_n)P=(p1​,p2​,...,pn​) of numbers {1,2,...,n}\{1, 2, ... , n\}{1,2,...,n}. Let us construct a graph G(P)G(P)G(P) with nnn vertices as follows: if i,j∈{1,...,n}i, j \in \{1, ..., n\}i,j∈{1,...,n} and i<ji < ji<j, then an undirected edge (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is present in G(P)G(P)G(P) if and only if pi<pjp_i < p_jpi​<pj​.

Given a permutation PPP, answer several queries of the form "what is the shortest length of a path between uuu and vvv in G(P)G(P)G(P)?". If there is no path between uuu and vvv, report so.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test case descriptions follow.
Each test case description starts with a line containing two integers, nnn and qqq, denoting the length of the permutation PPP and the number of queries respectively.
The next line contains nnn distinct integers p1,...,pnp_1, ..., p_np1​,...,pn​ denoting the permutation PPP.
The following qqq lines describe queries. The iii-th of these lines contains two integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ denoting parameters of the iii-th query.


Output Format

Print answers for all queries in order of appearance in the input, one per line.
If there is no path between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, print −1-1−1 as the answer for the iii-th query. Otherwise, print a single integer denoting the shortest length of a path between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains two integers n and q, denoting the length of the permutation and the number of queries respectively. The next line contains n distinct integers p1,...,pnp1,...,pn denoting the permutation. The following q lines describe queries. Each query line contains two integers ui and vi denoting the parameters of the query.
content.outputFormat: Print answers for all queries in order of appearance in the input, one per line. If there is no path between ui and vi, print -1 as the answer for the query. Otherwise, print a single integer denoting the shortest length of a path between ui and vi.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first query, we can go from 1 to 1 by just staying there, and hence path of length 0.
In the second query, we can go from the 2 (which has the value p2=5p_2 = 5p2​=5) to 1, because 1<21 < 21<2 and p1<p2p_1 < p_2p1​<p2​. From there, we can go to 4, and from there to 3. This gives us a path of length 3, and is the shortest that we can get.

In the fourth query, we can go from 4 to 5, because 4<45 < 45<5 and p4<p5p_4 < p_5p4​<p5​. This gives us a path of length 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph traversal algorithm to find the shortest path between each pair of nodes in the graph. We can represent the graph as an adjacency list, where each node is connected to its neighbors in the permutation. We can then use a breadth-first search (BFS) or depth-first search (DFS) algorithm to find the shortest path between each pair of nodes.
